name: axiom
description: Query Axiom datasets with APL and ingest events

envVars.name: AXIOM_TOKEN
envVars.description: Axiom API token with query (and optionally ingest) permissions

tools.name: List Datasets
tools.description: List datasets in the Axiom organization

tools.name: Run APL Query
tools.description: Run an APL (Axiom Processing Language) query across datasets

apl.description: APL query, e.g. ['my-dataset'] | where status == 500 | limit 50

startTime.description: Query window start — RFC3339 timestamp or relative expression like now-1h

endTime.description: Query window end — RFC3339 timestamp or relative expression like now

tools.name: Ingest Events
tools.description: Ingest a batch of JSON events into a dataset

events.description: Array of JSON event objects to ingest, e.g. [{"level":"info","message":"deploy finished"}]

prompts.title: Recent errors
prompts.prompt: Run an Axiom APL query for error-level events in my main dataset over the last hour and summarize them.

prompts.title: Dataset overview
prompts.prompt: List my Axiom datasets and tell me what kind of data each one holds.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Axiom API Setup

steps.title: Sign up for Axiom
steps.description: Create a free account at https://app.axiom.co — the free plan includes API access. Create a dataset and send a few test events so queries return data.

steps.title: Create an API token
steps.description: Go to Settings → API tokens (https://app.axiom.co/settings/api-tokens) and create a token. Grant query permission on the datasets you want to read, and ingest permission if you will use Ingest Events.

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add the token to your .env as AXIOM_TOKEN=...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Datasets, then Run APL Query with ['<dataset>'] | limit 10.

notes: Requests authenticate with 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' against https://api.axiom.co
APL queries POST to /v1/query/_apl; startTime/endTime accept RFC3339 timestamps or relative expressions like now-1d
API tokens have granular dataset permissions — a query-only token cannot ingest; personal access tokens additionally require an org ID header and are not used here
